speaker: SALARINO
text: Why, man, I saw Bassanio under sail;

speaker: SALARINO
text: With him is Gratiano gone along;

speaker: SALARINO
text: And in their ship I am sure Lorenzo is not.

speaker: SOLANIO
text: The villain Jew with outcries raised the Duke,

speaker: SOLANIO
text: Who went with him to search Bassanio’s ship.

speaker: SALARINO
text: He came too late; the ship was under sail.

speaker: SALARINO
text: But there the Duke was given to understand

speaker: SALARINO
text: That in a gondola were seen together

speaker: SALARINO
text: Lorenzo and his amorous Jessica.

speaker: SALARINO
text: Besides, Antonio certified the Duke

speaker: SALARINO
text: They were not with Bassanio in his ship.

speaker: SOLANIO
text: I never heard a passion so confused,

speaker: SOLANIO
text: So strange, outrageous, and so variable

speaker: SOLANIO
text: As the dog Jew did utter in the streets.

speaker: SOLANIO
text: “My daughter, O my ducats, O my daughter!

speaker: SOLANIO
text: Fled with a Christian! O my Christian ducats!

speaker: SOLANIO
text: Justice, the law, my ducats, and my daughter,

speaker: SOLANIO
text: A sealèd bag, two sealèd bags of ducats,

speaker: SOLANIO
text: Of double ducats, stol’n from me by my daughter,

speaker: SOLANIO
text: And jewels—two stones, two rich and precious stones—

speaker: SOLANIO
text: Stol’n by my daughter! Justice! Find the girl!

speaker: SOLANIO
text: She hath the stones upon her, and the ducats.”

speaker: SALARINO
text: Why, all the boys in Venice follow him,

speaker: SALARINO
text: Crying “His stones, his daughter, and his ducats.”

speaker: SOLANIO
text: Let good Antonio look he keep his day,

speaker: SOLANIO
text: Or he shall pay for this.

speaker: SALARINO
text: Marry, well remembered.

speaker: SALARINO
text: I reasoned with a Frenchman yesterday

speaker: SALARINO
text: Who told me, in the Narrow Seas that part

speaker: SALARINO
text: The French and English, there miscarrièd

speaker: SALARINO
text: A vessel of our country richly fraught.

speaker: SALARINO
text: I thought upon Antonio when he told me,

speaker: SALARINO
text: And wished in silence that it were not his.

speaker: SOLANIO
text: You were best to tell Antonio what you hear—

speaker: SOLANIO
text: Yet do not suddenly, for it may grieve him.

speaker: SALARINO
text: A kinder gentleman treads not the Earth.

speaker: SALARINO
text: I saw Bassanio and Antonio part.

speaker: SALARINO
text: Bassanio told him he would make some speed

speaker: SALARINO
text: Of his return. He answered “Do not so.

speaker: SALARINO
text: Slubber not business for my sake, Bassanio,

speaker: SALARINO
text: But stay the very riping of the time;

speaker: SALARINO
text: And for the Jew’s bond which he hath of me,

speaker: SALARINO
text: Let it not enter in your mind of love.

speaker: SALARINO
text: Be merry, and employ your chiefest thoughts

speaker: SALARINO
text: To courtship and such fair ostents of love

speaker: SALARINO
text: As shall conveniently become you there.”

speaker: SALARINO
text: And even there, his eye being big with tears,

speaker: SALARINO
text: Turning his face, he put his hand behind him,

speaker: SALARINO
text: And with affection wondrous sensible

speaker: SALARINO
text: He wrung Bassanio’s hand—and so they parted.

speaker: SOLANIO
text: I think he only loves the world for him.

speaker: SOLANIO
text: I pray thee, let us go and find him out

speaker: SOLANIO
text: And quicken his embracèd heaviness

speaker: SOLANIO
text: With some delight or other.

speaker: SALARINO
text: Do we so.
